findings.title: claimEarned pays fixed rewards immediately and can be repeated indefinitely for the same stake

best_agent_match.title: claimEarned can be called infinitely and does not enforce lock expiry

findings.title: Rewards claimed via claimEarned are paid again during unstake

best_agent_match.title: Rewards claimed through claimEarned are paid again during unstake

findings.title: Owner can arbitrarily sweep staked tokens and leave the pool insolvent

best_agent_match.title: Owner can arbitrarily drain the staking pool via penaltyWithdraw

findings.title: Core staking flows ignore ERC20 return values and can mutate accounting after failed token transfers

best_agent_match.title: User-facing token transfers ignore ERC20 return values and can silently corrupt accounting

rejected_candidates.title: Double counting bug in claimEarned totalRewards calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Confirmed, but totalRewards and related counters are not used to gate payouts or permissions anywhere in the contract; this is bookkeeping corruption only, not realistic protocol-level harm on its own.

rejected_candidates.title: claimEarned missing nonReentrant modifier
rejected_candidates.reason: Speculative and not needed to exploit the contract. The reportable issue is the missing reward-settlement logic itself; adding nonReentrant would not prevent the observed drain.

rejected_candidates.title: Stake amount can be reduced to zero without updating stakesCount
rejected_candidates.reason: Inaccurate or stale counting can waste gas and misreport pool metadata, but it does not create realistic fund loss, lockup, or protocol insolvency.

rejected_candidates.title: earnedToken rewards are not time-proportional
rejected_candidates.reason: The contract implements fixed-plan rewards paid on maturity, not linear vesting. Using apr as a label is misleading, but the code is internally consistent with fixed-per-plan payouts.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can permanently disable staking plans with no recovery
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner-controlled configuration choice for new deposits, not an exploitable vulnerability affecting existing funds.

rejected_candidates.title: emergencyWithdraw allows withdrawal during lock period
rejected_candidates.reason: The function name and logic indicate an intentional early-exit path. It does deduct 20% from the withdrawn amount, so the claim that users exit with no penalty is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: No event emitted for critical state changes
rejected_candidates.reason: Transparency issue only; not a reportable protocol-level vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Uninitialized staking struct pushed to array
rejected_candidates.reason: Solidity initializes the new struct to zero values before explicit field assignment. No exploitable behavior follows from this pattern here.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing input validation for _stakingId in claimEarned
rejected_candidates.reason: plans is a mapping, so out-of-range ids do not create unsafe memory access; invalid ids simply reference empty/default storage and do not create material harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Unused penalty state variable
rejected_candidates.reason: penalty is used in emergencyWithdraw. The commented burn transfer is better understood as part of the broader owner-custody issue already captured by penaltyWithdraw, not a separate standalone bug.
